headline: Building High-Resolution Art with Mechanical Flip-Discs
lede: Modern digital displays often lack the tactile soul of electromechanical hardware. This grid installation bridges that gap, pairing Nvidia-powered computation with the rhythmic clatter of physical flip-discs.

headline: Reviving Friendster as a Friction-Based Social Network
lede: A domain investor has purchased the iconic brand to force real-world interactions through phone-tapping. It is an intentional move to replace algorithmic convenience with the awkward reality of physical presence.

headline: Bob Odenkirk on Finding Purpose in a Meaningless Farce
lede: After a brush with mortality, the comedian peels back the curtain on his craft and the inherent absurdity of celebrity. It is a quiet study on how one manages personal truth while performing for the public gaze.

headline: Why Engineers Must Guard Their Cognitive Autonomy
lede: The temptation to outsource complex problem-solving to AI threatens to hollow out the foundational judgment of the engineering profession. We must treat these tools as assistants, not as replacements for the essential work of thinking.
pullquote: True technical depth is the final barrier against automated mediocrity

headline: Democratizing High-End Audio Tuning with Raspberry Pi
lede: The DSPi firmware project allows hobbyists to bypass costly commercial hardware for professional-grade parametric equalization. By unlocking the signal chain on cheap microcontrollers, it brings sophisticated room correction to the masses.

headline: Automating Documentation Assets During the Build Process
lede: Stop committing stale screenshots to your repository. This custom Rake task manages headless browser captures to ensure documentation remains synchronized with every UI update.

headline: Revisiting Data-Oblivious Quantization for Large Language Models
lede: I In the pursuit of efficient inference, researchers are revisiting vector rotation methods to compress high-dimensional data into low-bit representations. This approach seeks to normalize distribution variance without the computational overhead of traditional calibration.

headline: Running Gemini Nano Locally via the New Prompt API
lede: Google’s experimental browser API shifts the burden of generative intelligence from the cloud to the local GPU. It is a quiet, compute-heavy attempt to standardize how we embed privacy-preserving logic directly into the web.

headline: The Hidden Value Found in Abandoned Side Projects
lede: We often mistake a project's completion for its ultimate purpose. True utility is frequently found in the skills acquired during the development process, regardless of whether the final product is ever shipped.

headline: The Necessary Runtime Trade-offs of Reference Counting
lede: Rust’s static ownership model is the gold standard for safety, yet it struggles with the complexity of shared object graphs. We must acknowledge when the strictness of the compiler ends and the utility of reference counting begins.
